task: I’m closing out a shift and need to leave a quick note for the next person about a table that might come up again later. What’s a simple way to jot down the important details so someone else can follow what happened without having to ask me twice?

turns.agent_response:
Use a short, factual note with 4 parts:

**Table / time — issue — current status — next step**

Example:
**Table 12, 8:40 PM — guest disputed item on check and changed story about what was ordered — manager informed, ticket reviewed — if they return, confirm one version at a time and refer to manager.**

If you want, I can give you a **copy-paste note template** you can use every shift.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the shape of it. I’d want it even tighter though.

- table number
- what they said / did
- what you already checked
- what the next person needs to watch for

Something like:

**Tbl 12 — guest questioned bill after extra apps/drinks; ticket and handwritten order match; manager aware; if they come back, don’t re-open without checking receipts.**

Keep it factual. No little story, no attitude. Just enough so the next person doesn’t walk in blind.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — keep it to one line:

**Tbl __ — guest said/did __; checked __; next shift: __.**

Example:
**Tbl 12 — guest questioned bill after extra apps/drinks; ticket and handwritten order match; manager aware; next shift: don’t re-open without checking receipts.**

If you want, I can make you a few **super-short fill-in templates** for different situations.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. One line, no extra noise. Good enough to hand off without starting a whole second problem.
